pattern: Guaranteeing a win at minimum cost is an interval game: for each range you pick the guess whose worst-case cost (the guess price plus the costlier of the two resulting sub-ranges) is smallest, solved by dynamic programming over interval lengths.
hints: You must survive the unluckiest answer, so each guess costs its value plus the worse of the two ranges it splits the search into.
Define the cost to guarantee a win over a contiguous range, and build it up from short ranges to longer ones.
For a range from lo to hi, try every pivot k inside it: the cost is k plus the maximum of solving lo..k-1 and k+1..hi, and you take the minimum such cost over all k; empty ranges cost zero.

edge_inputs.description: single number costs nothing to guess
two numbers, guess the smaller first
small range where greedy middle is not optimal

stress.description: random range size across the legal span
stress.note: interval DP is quadratic so the full range stays fast
